label: Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes

node.label: Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes
node.definition: Code-execution environments parse and run generated programs inside interpreters, containers, virtual machines, or restricted sandboxes with observable results.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes is to connect Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Caches, APIs & tool interfaces to downstream capabilities including Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops, Human-in-the-loop, permissions & sandboxing, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence.
examples: substrate for Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
substrate for Code models, copilots & repository intelligence
limitations: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations, Caches, APIs & tool interfaces.

dependencies.rationale: Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes uses Programs, ASTs, bytecode & intermediate representations as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes uses Caches, APIs & tool interfaces as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Code execution, interpreters & sandboxes uses Constraint propagation, logic & program execution as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
